channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: Ring of Fire roads to be complete by 2031, ahead of schedule: Ford
Ontario teacher, education worker unions call for contract talks to start early
item.description: Contracts for Ontario’s teachers and education workers expire at the end of this summer and their union leaders are calling on the education minister to start the bargaining process early, saying issues such as class sizes need to be urgently addressed.

item.title: Ontario and Nova Scotia to allow direct-to-consumer alcohol sales between provinces
item.description: Ontario and Nova Scotia will begin allowing consumers to buy alcohol directly from producers in each province as both jurisdictions look for ways to increase internal trade within Canada in the face of the U.S. trade war.

item.title: Plans in works to replace Metro Toronto Convention Centre, Ford says
item.description: Toronto could soon be getting a modern new convention centre to replace its marquee convention space, Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Tuesday.

item.title: Ontario’s plan to increase the powers of special constables drawing mixed reviews
item.description: Ontario’s plan to give special constables additional powers, allowing them to arrest and charge people caught using drugs in public places, is drawing mixed reviews.

item.title: Doug Ford takes issue with Toronto's 13-year timeline to build road safety measures
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford took issue Thursday with a City of Toronto report that concluded it would take upwards of 13 years and cost $52 million to build speed bumps and other traffic calming measures in school zones.

item.title: Ontario reveals design for new science centre at Toronto’s waterfront, says it could be ready in 3 years
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford pulled back the curtain Thursday on the province’s design for a new science centre at Toronto’s waterfront, calling it a “world-class” facility that will be “jaw-dropping.”

item.title: ‘I hope they hurt’: Unifor irate over early idling of Crown Royal factory in Amherstburg, Ont.
item.description: Unifor is upset over the early idling of the Crown Royal factory in Amherstburg, Ont.

item.title: Black trustees concerned Ontario school board supervision weakening anti-racism fight
item.description: Provincial school board takeovers in Ontario are weakening the governance needed to track and end anti-Black racism in schools, Black trustees are warning.

item.title: Doug Ford’s OSAP changes leave some students ‘genuinely distraught’
item.description: Students in Ontario say they’re prepared to challenge the changes to post-secondary tuition and financial aid announced by Doug Ford’s government last week.

item.title: Province breaks ground on elevated stretch of Ontario Line, with possible opening date of ‘early 2030s’
item.description: The Ontario government has broken ground on the “elevated guideway and four new stations” for the Ontario Line, marking a major construction milestone on the long-promised subway project.

item.title: Students disappointed by Ontario’s cuts to OSAP grants, tuition freeze lift
item.description: Post-secondary students in Ontario say the government’s decision to reduce financial assistance grants while lifting a long-standing tuition fee freeze will leave students who are already struggling financially with more debt.

item.title: Skills Development Fund recipient countersues Ontario government, alleging reputational harm
item.description: A mental health company being sued by the Ontario government over alleged fraudulent misrepresentation has filed a statement of defence and a counterclaim against the province, alleging it has damaged the company’s reputation.

item.title: $23M deal reached to keep Crown Royal on LCBO shelves
item.description: Crown Royal will remain on LCBO shelves after its manufacturer committed to $23 million in new investments in Ontario.

item.title: Ontario lifting post-secondary tuition freeze, boosting funding
item.description: Ontario is giving colleges and universities billions in new funding, while lifting a seven-year tuition fee freeze and cutting back on the amount of student assistance grants.
